seoTitle: How to Search for a Word in Google Docs Fast (2026)
metaDescription: Find any word or phrase in Google Docs instantly using the search shortcut, then use Find and Replace to update text across the whole document.
intro: Scrolling through long documents to find one phrase wastes time and risks missing something. This guide covers Google Docs' built-in search shortcut and the more advanced Find and Replace tool, so you can locate and update text across an entire document in seconds.

steps.heading: Open the quick search bar
steps.body: Press Ctrl+F (or Cmd+F on Mac) while your document is open to launch the floating search bar in the top right corner.

steps.heading: Type your search term
steps.body: Enter the word or phrase you're looking for, and Google Docs will highlight every matching instance in the document.

steps.heading: Navigate between matches
steps.body: Use the up and down arrows next to the search field to jump from one highlighted match to the next.

steps.heading: Open Find and Replace for bulk edits
steps.body: Click the three-dot menu next to the search bar and select Find and Replace to swap a word or phrase throughout the document.

steps.heading: Fine-tune with match case and replace all
steps.body: Check 'Match case' if you need exact capitalization, then use Replace or Replace All to update every occurrence at once.

faq.question: Can I search inside comments and footnotes?
faq.answer: The basic search bar focuses on the main body text, so comments and footnotes may not always surface in results.

faq.question: Is there a way to undo a Replace All action?
faq.answer: Yes, pressing Ctrl+Z (or Cmd+Z) right after a bulk replace will revert the changes.

faq.question: Does search work on mobile?
faq.answer: The Google Docs mobile app has its own search icon in the toolbar that opens a similar find interface.

faq.question: Can I search for exact phrases with spaces?
faq.answer: Yes, typing a multi-word phrase into the search bar will match that exact sequence of words.
